Session Management.details: Initializes a new session and attaches to it, providing a terminal multiplexer environment.
Creates a new tmux session with a specific name, making it easier to identify and manage multiple sessions.
Starts a new named session in the background without automatically attaching to it.
Displays a list of all currently active tmux sessions along with their status.
Connects your current terminal to the most recently active tmux session.
Connects to a specific tmux session by its name.
Terminates a specific tmux session and all its windows and panes.
Completely stops the tmux server, closing all active sessions.
Changes the name of an existing tmux session.
Switches the current client to another active tmux session.
Disconnects from the current tmux session while leaving it running in the background.

Windows.details: Opens a new window within the current tmux session.
Prompts to rename the current window for better organization.
Moves focus to the next window in the current session's sequence.
Moves focus to the previous window in the current session's sequence.

Windows.desc: Switch to the last active window
Windows.details: Quickly toggles between the current window and the previously active one.
Prompts to confirm and then closes the currently active window.
Displays an interactive list of all windows in all sessions.
Allows searching for a window by name or content.
Prompts for a window index to move the current window to.

Windows.desc: Switch to a window by number
Windows.details: Directly jumps to the window corresponding to the pressed digit.

Panes.details: Divides the current pane into two side-by-side (left and right).
Divides the current pane into two stacked (top and bottom).
Prompts to confirm and then closes the currently active pane.
Expands the current pane to fill the entire window, or shrinks it back if already zoomed.
Moves focus to the next pane in the current window.
Moves focus to the pane in the specified direction (Up, Down, Left, Right).

Panes.desc: Switch to the last active pane
Panes.details: Quickly toggles focus between the current pane and the previously active one.
Briefly displays numbers over each pane; pressing a number switches focus to it.
Moves the current pane to the previous position in the pane list.
Moves the current pane to the next position in the pane list.
Cycles the window through predefined pane arrangements (e.g., even-horizontal, main-vertical).

Panes.desc: Break pane into a new window
Panes.details: Moves the current pane out into its own new window.
Resizes the current pane in the specified direction by five units.
Marks a pane for later use with commands like 'swap-pane'.

Copy Mode.details: Enables scrolling through terminal history and selecting text to copy.
Begins selecting text from the current cursor position in copy mode.
Copies the selected text into the tmux buffer and exits copy mode.
Pastes the contents of the most recently copied tmux buffer into the current pane.
Starts a forward search for a string within the terminal history while in copy mode.
Starts a backward search for a string within the terminal history while in copy mode.
Moves the cursor to the next occurrence of the search term.
Moves the cursor to the previous occurrence of the search term.

Configuration.details: Opens a prompt for entering tmux commands directly.
Displays a list of all currently configured tmux key bindings.
Lists all bound keys and the commands they execute from the shell.
Enables mouse support for selecting panes, windows, and scrolling.
Reloads the tmux settings from the configuration file without restarting.
Displays a log of recent status messages and alerts.
